title: The Farmer and the Stork
lines: A Farmer set traps in his field to catch Cranes that were eating his grain.
One morning he found that he had caught a Stork along with the Cranes.
"I am not a Crane," said the Stork; "I am a Stork, a noble and pious bird."
"Do not kill me with these disreputable Cranes."
"I am sorry," replied the Farmer, "but you were found in bad company, and I cannot trust you to be different from the Cranes."
moral: Bad company leads to bad reputation.
